title: Reader Outcomes, Repository Baseline, Static Architecture, and Evidence · Program Brief: Evidence Ladder
summary: A team says “build a blog generator” but has not defined readers, publishing authority, content ownership, supported routes, accessibility needs, repository state, or acceptance evidence. Predict the reader decision, source identity, parse state, output URL, accessibility result, failure, and evidence before reading the governing source. Planned artifact: Add and verify the evidence ladder increment in reader, repository, content, architecture, and evidence charter. Learners progress through pr…
objectives: Separate parser, renderer, file-tree, browser, accessibility, deployment, cache, and stakeholder evidence.
Record language, reading, keyboard, reflow, motion, media, cognitive, and assistive-technology needs as build inputs.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

starterFiles.python:
"""build-static-site-python cumulative production static-site generator portfolio.

Browser execution uses deterministic pure-Python parser, route, render, graph, and publication models
with original fixed in-memory fixtures only. Native filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py,
Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production effects
require controlled transfer gates.
"""

from dataclasses import dataclass


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class PublicationPolicy:
    base_path: str = "/handbook/"
    encoding: str = "utf-8"
    raw_html_allowed: bool = False
    max_document_bytes: int = 262_144
    max_nesting_depth: int = 64


@dataclass(frozen=True)
class BuildEvidence:
    revision: str
    content_manifest_digest: str
    route_manifest_digest: str
    artifact_digest: str
    accessibility_review_id: str


def portfolio_ready() -> str:
    return "Python static-site generator portfolio ready"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Predict Evidence Ladder · Theory Evidence Ladder
steps.instruction:
theory case — A theory publishing team handles case 2601 during a compromised-content recovery rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the publication charter on reader value, repository state, accessibility, and content authority; reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval; then insert malformed nested delimiters and retain later valid content with a located diagnostic. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate parser, renderer, file-tree, browser, accessibility, deployment, cache, and stakeholder evidence.

Before reading, predict which action preserves the Evidence Ladder contract for the supplied data.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-worked-static-site-outcomes-repo-architecture-4
# Competency: ssg-worked-static-site-outcomes-repo-architecture-4.
# Case 1655: an accessibility-led community newsroom release.
# Operating constraint: reconcile content, routes, navigation, links, assets, feeds, sitemaps, output files, and artifact digests before approval.
# Changed case: delete a source and change a shared layout, then compare incremental and clean artifact digests.
def worked_static_site_outcomes_repo_architecture_4(reader_task: str = "find-current-guidance", acceptance_cases: int = 8, repository_clean: bool = True, accessibility_plan: bool = True, content_authorized: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_worked4 = "worked4-4dekrow-1655"
    assert evidence_variant_worked4
    evidence_axes_worked4 = (
        "readerworked4",
        "source1655",
        "parse4dekrow",
        "routeworked41655",
        "artifact1655worked4",
        "recovery4dekrow1655",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_worked4)) == 6
    if not reader_task.strip() or acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-outcome"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not accessibility_plan:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "accessibility-gap"
    if not content_authorized:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "content-authority"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 4
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-site-charter"
content.caption: Runnable deterministic pure-Python static-site decision model; trace one accepted and one rejected source, parse, route, render, asset, accessibility, cache, deployment, or recovery case, then state which filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, or production claim still needs controlled transfer evidence.

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Build Evidence Ladder evidence · Theory Evidence Ladder
steps.instruction:

Browser code must not claim native filesystem, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validator, browser, GitHub, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN, cache, or production behavior; verify those boundaries later with Python 3.14.6, CommonMark 0.31.2 fixtures, markdown-it-py 4.2.0, Jinja 3.1.6 strict autoescape, pytest 9.1.1, standards validators, real browsers, clean package installs, reproducible artifacts, staged deployment, rollback, recovery, and release gates.

content.code:
# Evidence: ssg-evidence-ladder-1mbd68
# Competency: ssg-evidence-ladder.
# Case 8691: a compromised-content recovery rehearsal.
# Operating constraint: record the first failed invariant, repair, regression evidence, reader task, rollback, recovery, and residual-risk owner.
# Changed case: zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks.
def evidence_ssg_evidence_ladder_1mbd68(reader_task: str = "find-current-guidance", acceptance_cases: int = 8, repository_clean: bool = True, accessibility_plan: bool = True, content_authorized: bool = True) -> tuple[bool, int | float, str]:
    evidence_variant_1mbd68 = "1mbd68-86dbm1-8691"
    assert evidence_variant_1mbd68
    evidence_axes_1mbd68 = (
        "reader1mbd68",
        "source8691",
        "parse86dbm1",
        "route1mbd688691",
        "artifact86911mbd68",
        "recovery86dbm18691",
    )
    assert len(set(evidence_axes_1mbd68)) == 6
    if not reader_task.strip() or acceptance_cases < 4:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "undefined-outcome"
    if not repository_clean:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "repository-baseline"
    if not accessibility_plan:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "accessibility-gap"
    if not content_authorized:
        return False, acceptance_cases, "content-authority"
    assert acceptance_cases >= 4
    return True, acceptance_cases, "bounded-site-charter"

steps.title: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Evidence Ladder model · Theory Evidence Ladder
steps.instruction:
Write the theory evidence note for Evidence Ladder: explain the governing condition, one failure, and how this concrete case was verified — A theory publishing team handles case 1887 during a compromised-content recovery rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the publication charter on reader value, repository state, accessibility, and content authority; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate parser, renderer, file-tree, browser, accessibility, deployment, cache, and stakeholder evidence.

Evidence target: Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Evidence Ladder model · Theory Evidence Ladder.
steps.why: Retrieval and explanation expose gaps that recognition questions can hide. For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Evidence Ladder model · Theory Evidence Ladder, keep Evidence Ladder connected to executable Python source, parse, route, render, accessibility, artifact, deployment, and recovery decision evidence.

content.title: Evidence defense
content.text: Use the case, one assumption, one observable result, and one limitation. Do not copy the competency statement: Separate parser, renderer, file-tree, browser, accessibility, deployment, cache, and stakeholder evidence.

At Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Evidence Ladder model · Theory Evidence Ladder, trace this concrete input and predict admission, decoding, tokenization, parsing, transformation, routing, rendering, validation, publication, failure, rollback, and recovery evidence before reviewing it: A theory publishing team handles case 1887 during a compromised-content recovery rehearsal. Build deterministic Python evidence to gate the publication charter on reader value, repository state, accessibility, and content authority; keep browser work pure, deterministic, bounded, and free of native filesystem, template engine, validator, network, GitHub, DNS, TLS, CDN, and host effects; then zoom a translated long-form page to 400 percent at mobile width with images and code blocks. Browser code uses pure bounded models and original fixed fixtures only. Real filesystems, full CommonMark, markdown-it-py, Jinja, validators, browsers, GitHub Actions, Pages, DNS, TLS, CDN caches, and production behavior require explicit controlled transfer gates. Competency probe: Separate parser, renderer, file-tree, browser, accessibility, deployment, cache, and stakeholder evidence.

For Static-site publishing and Python Defend the Evidence Ladder model · Theory Evidence Ladder, challenge the documented misconception with changed-source, delimiter, Unicode, nesting, route, URL, asset, viewport, cache, security, deployment, recovery, and controlled production-transfer evidence: A visually correct local page proves the published site is complete.
